messages.content: When was the battle of plassey fought?

The Battle of Plassey was fought on June 23, 1757. It was a decisive victory for the British East India Company and marked the beginning of British rule in India. The battle was fought between the forces of the East India Company and the ruler of Bengal, Siraj-ud-Daula. The East India Company emerged victorious and was able to establish control over the region. The battle is considered a turning point in the history of the British Empire in India.

The Battle of Plassey was fought between the forces of the British East India Company and the ruler of Bengal, Siraj-ud-Daula. The British East India Company was led by Robert Clive, while Siraj-ud-Daula was accompanied by his army, which included both Indian and European soldiers. The East India Company's army consisted primarily of European soldiers, but also included some Indian soldiers who were loyal to the company. The exact number of soldiers on each side is not known, but it is estimated that the East India Company had around 1,500 soldiers while Siraj-ud-Daula had around 5,000.
